items.title: India’s textile sector at an inflection point as global tariff barriers ease; Arvind Fashion, Raymond Lifestyle top buys
items.description: The immediate reduction in US tariffs on Indian textile goods from 50% to 18% has brought long-awaited relief to an industry that had been grappling with margin compression, regulatory costs, and weakening demand—particularly in home textiles, where the market had declined by around 4% and smaller players were nearing shutdown.

items.title: From 1929 to today: what history tells us about America’s speculative culture and India’s prudence
items.description: The American financial system is structurally designed to accommodate, and often actively encourage, speculative behaviour. Indian markets, by contrast, are deliberately constructed to restrain it.

items.title: Silver ETFs crash 38% in 7 days. Time to buy for those who missed the rally?
items.description: Silver exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have plunged about 38% from their record high reached just seven trading sessions earlier on January 29, as higher margin requirements and profit-booking led to a sharp unwinding of leveraged bets.

items.title: 11 penny stocks surged up to 300% in 3 months. Do you own any?
items.description: In the last three months, 11 penny stocks delivered impressive gains ranging from 10% to 308%. We identified these outperformers using specific filters: a market capitalisation below Rs 1,000 crore, a share price under Rs 20, and a minimum trading volume of 5 lakh shares. This approach helped spotlight low-priced, actively traded micro-cap stocks showing strong upward momentum.Penny stocks continue to draw interest due to their low price points and high return potential. However, they come with substantial risk, marked by low liquidity, sharp volatility, and limited transparency. For investors, success in this space requires more than luck; it demands discipline, thorough research, and a firm grip on risk management. (Data Source: ACE Equity)

items.title: US trade deal framework to boost investor confidence, strength capital flows, deepen markets: BSE chief
items.description: The successful conclusion of an interim trade framework agreement between India and the US will boost investor confidence, strengthen the foundation for capital flows, and deepen market participation as India integrates further with the world economy--directly advancing the vision of Viksit Bharat, said Sundararaman Ramamurthy, MD and CEO, Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE).

items.title: FPIs turn net buyers in Feb; invest Rs 8,100 cr in a week on US trade deal
items.description: After three consecutive months of heavy selling, foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) turned net buyers in the first week of February, infusing more than Rs 8,100 crore in Indian equities, aided by improving risk sentiment, along with a trade deal with the US.

items.title: Mcap of 8 of top 10 valued firms surges by whopping Rs 4.55 lakh cr; Reliance biggest winner
items.description: The combined market valuation of eight of the top 10 valued firms jumped by a whopping Rs 4.55 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries emerging as the biggest winner, in line with a remarkable rally in equities.

items.title: Dividend yield stocks as tactical & trading plays in a volatile market: 4 stocks with dividend yields of up to 6.1%
items.description: The Nifty and Sensex are likely to remain volatile as we enter a phase of tight liquidity ahead of the financial year ending. And given that a New World Order is in the making, this volatility may well continue. So, how to deal with volatility? One way is to use a mix of conventional and unconventional methods to ensure that if the market stays under pressure and stocks are not creating wealth, they should be at least generating income. In short, the dividend play.

items.title: 10 largecap stocks with massive upside potential of up to 45%. Do you own any?
items.description: Analyst forecasts offer more than just numbers — they provide a strategic view of future market potential. For investors seeking the next big opportunity, a closer examination of BSE Large-Cap stocks highlights some promising contenders.Based on consensus estimates from Trendlyne, a number of large-cap stocks are projected to deliver strong returns over the next 12 months. This anticipated “upside” represents the average expected gain over the coming year, providing a data-driven benchmark for investors targeting high-potential large-cap opportunities. In this analysis, we spotlight eight standout large-cap stocks expected to deliver gains in the range of 25% to 45% over the year ahead.

items.title: Cement majors post double-digit volume growth in Q3, expect price recovery ahead
items.description: Leading cement makers reported strong double-digit year-on-year growth in sales volumes during the December 2025 quarter, even as their realisations came under pressure.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday
items.description: Indian benchmark indices closed higher on the back of buying interest in consumer and private banking stocks, though gains were limited by selling pressure across auto, IT and pharma names.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy HUL and JSW Steel on Monday for up to 8% short term gains. Here’s why
items.description: Nifty closed higher on the back of buying interest in consumer and private banking stocks, though gains were limited by selling pressure across auto, IT and pharma names. The index has been consolidating over the last few sessions.Commenting on the key trends, Rupak De, Senior Technical Analyst at LKP Securities said that the support lies at 25,500 on the lower end while the resistance is seen around 25,700 on the higher end. "In the short term, the index is likely to remain range-bound between 25,500 and 25,700. A decisive breakout on either side of this range could trigger a directional move," De said.Here are 2 stock recommendations for Monday:
